japanese_name: カルビー ピザポテト
description: Calbee Pizza Potato. Cheese-dusted potato chips with little melty topping bits. The konbini bag for people who want pizza energy without the social contract of pizza.

japanese_name: 書道体験
description: Calligraphy lesson. Brush, ink, and one kanji that humbles everyone immediately. Tokyo and Kyoto studios make it an easy rainy-day win.

japanese_name: カルピス / カルピコ
description: Calpis. Sweet milky soda-adjacent drink with a light tang. The vending-machine pick when water feels too responsible.

japanese_name: キャンドゥ 100円雑貨
description: Can Do 100-yen finds. Tiny organizers, stickers, and kitchen things that solve problems you invented in the aisle. Cheap enough to become a personality trait.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: pouches, stickers, kitchen clips, tiny organizers]; No reliable direct image found

japanese_name: キャンメイク クリームチーク
description: Canmake cosmetics. Cheap drugstore makeup that looks way less cheap than the receipt. Cream Cheek is the easy starter item.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Cream Cheek, Mermaid Skin Gel UV, Marshmallow Finish Powder, Muchi Puru Tint]

japanese_name: キャンメイク クリームチーク
description: Canmake Cream Cheek. Tiny drugstore blush with real color payoff. The budget J-beauty pick for a pouch that already has 400 lip products.

japanese_name: キャンメイク マーメイドスキンジェルUV
description: Canmake Mermaid Skin Gel UV. SPF50+ sunscreen gel that feels light, not chalky. Drugstore sunscreen for people who hate sunscreen.

japanese_name: 鉄のたまご焼き kokotama
description: Oigen kokotama iron tamagoyaki pan. Heavy little rectangle for serious omelet energy. Buy it when breakfast needs main-character treatment.

japanese_name: セザンヌ リップカラーシールド
description: CEZANNE cosmetics. Drugstore makeup with tiny prices and shockingly calm packaging. Lip Color Shield is the safe first grab.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Lip Color Shield, Make Keep Base, Natural Cheek N, UV Tone Up Base]

japanese_name: セザンヌ リップカラーシールド
description: CEZANNE Lip Color Shield. A ¥660 gel-film lip tint that looks more expensive than its life choices. Easy drugstore pickup, zero drama.

japanese_name: キャラクター菓子缶
description: Character snack tins. Cookies or candy in a tin people keep forever and call storage. Good for kids, coworkers, and your inner raccoon.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Sanrio cookie tins, Pokemon snack tins, Disney tins, Ghibli tins]; No verified source or image

japanese_name: キャラクター歯ブラシ
description: Character toothbrushes. Tiny travel toothbrushes wearing Sanrio, Pokemon, or Anpanman branding. Useful, cute, and somehow cheaper than coffee.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Sanrio toothbrushes, Pokemon toothbrushes, Anpanman toothbrushes, Skater toothbrush sets]; No verified source or image

japanese_name: 格安ハードスーツケース
description: Cheap hard-shell suitcase. Extra luggage for the final boss called 'I bought too much.' Buy it near the end, not day two.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Donki hardshell suitcase, expansion zipper carry case, lightweight zipper carry case, front-door carry case]; No verified source or image

japanese_name: かぶりもの / コスチューム
description: Donki animal hat aisle. Instant costume, low dignity, high photo yield. Grab one when the night needs a penguin head for no reason.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: animal hats, Halloween headwear, kigurumi, party masks]; No reliable direct image found

japanese_name: 格安ノーブランド電化製品
description: Cheap no-name electronics. The price looks brave; the charger looks like a lawsuit. Stick to known brands for adapters and heat tools.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: USB chargers, plug adapters, extension cords, travel hair tools]; No verified single SKU

japanese_name: 名入れ箸 / 若狭塗箸 / 江戸箸
description: Engraved chopsticks. A compact souvenir that does not scream airport panic. Wakasa-nuri and Edo Hashi shops turn names into an actually useful gift.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: Wakasa-nuri name engraving, Edo Hashi name engraving, personalized chopsticks]; No reliable direct image found

japanese_name: マルチタオルクリップ
description: Sanrio multiuse towel clip. Clips towels, straps, and kid gear before it hits a train floor. Parents buy one; everyone else buys three because Kuromi.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: towel clip, pacifier strap, bag strap clip]; Product source from Daiso Singapore; No reliable direct image found

japanese_name: アタックZERO パーフェクトスティック
description: Attack ZERO Perfect Stick. Pre-measured laundry detergent sticks, not a bottle of hotel-room chaos. Toss one in when coin laundry becomes your whole personality.
notes: [AMBIGUOUS: laundry detergent sheets, detergent pods, Attack ZERO Perfect Stick]

japanese_name: 駅ロッカー / マルチエキューブ
description: Eki-locker. Station lockers that save you from suitcase drag. Use them before temples, shopping streets, and every stair-heavy bad decision.

japanese_name: コインホーム MG-03
description: COIN HOME MG-03. Yen coins snap into their own little lanes. This is how you stop handing cashiers a tragic fistful of metal.

japanese_name: がま口小銭入れ
description: Gamaguchi coin purse. Tiny clasp pouch for the yen avalanche. Buy one before the 1-yen coins start reproducing.
notes: [CATEGORY_INPUT: gamaguchi coin purse, zipper coin pouch, travel coin case]; No verified source or image

japanese_name: ざるそば
description: 7-Eleven zaru soba tray. Cold noodles, dipping sauce, tiny wasabi packet, tiny chaos. The summer konbini lunch for people trying to feel functional.

japanese_name: 資生堂 ザ・コラーゲン ドリンク
description: Shiseido The Collagen Drink. Beauty drink in a tiny bottle with drugstore-auntie energy. People grab it before flights, baths, and optimistic skincare routines.

japanese_name: 手ぬぐい
description: Tenugui-style compact towel. Thin cotton towel that dries fast and folds flat. Carry it for temples, summer sweat, and bathrooms with no paper towels.

japanese_name: メディキュット
description: MediQtto compression socks. Drugstore leg squeeze for flights and all-day walking. Put them on before your calves file a complaint.
